notes: SHIP v1.14.207 — FIX: Command deploy tile unclickable on laptop/desktop (owner-reported; root-caused in desktop Chrome via browser automation). ROOT CAUSE: #pg-cmd .tile (the DEPLOY/SCANNED/HANDOFF shift tiles) inherited aspect-ratio:1/1.6 from the GENERIC .tile rule @8415 — the id-scoped #pg-cmd .tile rule @8856 overrides bg/border/shadow/layout but never RESET aspect-ratio, so it leaked through. On a narrow phone the tiles are ~110px wide -> ~176px tall (fits, never noticed). On a wide laptop the tiles are ~296px wide -> aspect 1/1.6 -> ~474px TALL, ballooning the Command page to ~1432px and pushing the tile row far below the fold, buried under the fixed bottom nav = the deploy tile is off-screen/unclickable. The handler (cmd_route(work,deploy)->showWorkTab) was always fine; the tile was just unreachable. FIX: add aspect-ratio:auto to #pg-cmd .tile — tiles return to their designed min-height:96px on ALL viewports (verified live: tile 474->96px, page 1432->1055px, deploy tile becomes on-screen + elementFromPoint reaches it). Diagnosis method: Chrome automation — enumerated deploy entry points (Work card deploy_quick / Command tile cmd_route / legacy), proved handlers + click-delivery fine, traced the tile height to aspect-ratio, tested the reset live before shipping. NOT a regression from the recent glass work — pre-existing latent bug exposed by desktop testing. VISUAL NOTE for device verify: on PHONE the shift tiles get slightly shorter (their intended 96px, was ~176px) — expected, correct. #pg-cmd is redesign-only (legacy never renders it) so ?legacy=1 unaffected. ROLLBACK: remove the aspect-ratio:auto. Gates: 4 blocks compile, braces balanced, CRLF, lockstep .206 -> .207.
